title: ligusticum kellerianum
intro: Ligusticum kellerianum, also known as Keller's lovage, is a plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Western North America and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems contain compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, and digestive problems. Ligusticum kellerianum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and its health benefits include reducing stress, improving sleep quality, and providing relief from muscle and joint pain.
